title: linaria multipunctata
intro: Linaria multipunctata, also known as Toadflax, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the figwort family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in the relief of sore throats. To use Linaria multipunctata, herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures can be made from the plant's leaves and flowers.
